channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: DOGE just announced more layoffs at TTS, the government’s vaunted tech group, sources say
item.description: Thomas Shedd, who directs the General Services Administration’s vaunted tech group, Technology Transformation Services (TTS), has just announced that a new round of firings has begun at TTS, insiders tells Fast Company.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Hackers breach Morocco's social security database in an unprecedented cyberattack
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco 's social security agency said troves of data were stolen from its systems in a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the messaging app Telegram.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Yes, Nintendo Switch 2 Edition games will contain the upgrades on the cartridge, but it seems Breath of the Wild DLC will still need to be purchased separately
item.description: Nintendo has confirmed that Switch 2 Edition games will contain the full game and upgrades on the cartridge, but there's uncertainty surrounding DLC.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nintendo says its divisive Game-Key Cards will help 'future-proof' the Switch 2
item.description: Nintendo wants to ensure that the Switch 2 is "future-proof", and it's trying to achieve that with its new Game-Key Cards.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: WhatsApp users fume over new Meta AI button that you can’t remove – here’s what it does
item.description: WhatsApp has rolled out its controversial Meta AI chatbot update in more countries – and many users aren't happy.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: Is Microsoft getting truly desperate with adverts now? I’m seriously unimpressed with a new ‘suggestion’ to buy Avowed in Windows 10 and Windows 11
item.description: Avowed might be good, but I don’t want Windows 10 shoving adverts in my face urging me to buy the game, thanks Microsoft.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The Pop Star Emojis Rumor: A Viral Falsehood - Apple's Next iOS Update Won't Feature Celebrity Emojis
item.description: A viral post claiming Apple would bring pop star emojis to iOS 18.5 has been debunked. The rumor originated from a parody account on X, which posted convincing images of celebrity emojis.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The Trump Administration Takes Aim at Christopher Krebs and CISA Over Allegations of Misconduct and Bias in Cybersecurity - A Directive with Far-Reaching Implications
item.description: The Trump administration has issued a directive against Christopher Krebs, the former head of the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), for alleged misconduct and bias during his tenure. The directive calls for a review of CISA's activities over the past six years and suspends Krebs' security clearance.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How does the Nintendo Switch 2 stack up against gaming handhelds like the Steam Deck and Rog Ally X? Find out in our latest podcast episode
item.description: On the latest episode of the TechRadar Podcast, we discuss the Switch 2, tarriffs, the rumored iPhone 17 Air and Microsoft's 50th birthday celebrations.
item.dc:creator: josephine.watson@futurenet.com (Josephine Watson), TechRadar

item.title: Google accused of harming kids by secretly grabbing data from school-provided tech products
item.description: Nearly 70% of U.S. schools use Google's products in classrooms, lawsuit says, and numerous Bay Area school districts do, too.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Xbox Games Showcase Returns on June 8: Here's What to Expect
item.description: The Xbox Games Showcase returns this summer, and it'll be followed by a special The Outer Worlds 2 Direct. Get ready for a new look at upcoming titles from across Xbox's first-party studios and third-party partners.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Gigabit NBN plans are cheaper, faster and better than ever – these are 4 I'd recommend
item.description: Fast speeds, low costs and plenty of extra perks, NBN 1000 plans are becoming increasingly more attractive.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Cisco, Google Cloud offer enterprises new way to connect SD-WANs
item.description: Cisco and Google Cloud have expanded their partnership to integrate Cisco’s SD-WAN with the cloud provider’s fully managed Cloud WAN service.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Shopify CEO: No new hires, unless you prove AI can't do the job
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, The Hill

item.title: Meta helped build China’s DeepSeek: Whistleblower testimony
item.description: Former Meta executive Sarah Wynn-Williams is set to testify before the US Senate Judiciary Committee on Wednesday, revealing how the company’s AI model, Llama, played a critical role in accelerating China’s AI capabilities — particularly contributing to the rise of DeepSeek.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: YouTube entrepreneur MrBeast slams Trump's 'brutal' tariffs
item.description: YouTube entrepreneur and star MrBeast slammed President Trump’s “brutal” tariffs in a post on the social platform X on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: World’s first AI-powered industrial super-humanoid robot
item.description: Mech the super-humanoid robot can lift up to 132 pounds and is designed to tackle stressful and repetitive tasks that often lead to workplace injuries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An AI avatar tried to argue a case before a New York court. The judges weren't having it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took only seconds for the judges on a New York appeals court to realize that the man addressing them from a video screen — a person about to present an argument in a lawsuit — not only had no law degree, but didn't exist at all.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4
